gloss_en: How are you today? What's happening at the market?
notes: šlōnak greeting, šaku māku idiom (erwin1963 §intro; blanc1964); monophthong ō in شلون

gloss_en: The weather is very hot in Baghdad in the summer.
notes: hwāya 'very' (erwin1963 s.v.); ج=/dʒ/; geminates وّ رّ; emphatic ص with backing

sentence: مَا أَرِيد قَهْوَة، خَلِّي نِشْرَب چَاي بِالْهَيل.
raw: ما أريد قهوة، خلي نشرب چاي بالهيل.
ipa: ˈmaː ʔaˈriːd ˈɡahwa ˈxalliː ˈniʃrab ˈtʃaːj bilˈheːl
gloss_en: I don't want coffee, let's drink tea with cardamom.
notes: ق=/ɡ/ gilit reflex in gahwa (blanc1964 §3.25); چ=/tʃ/ čāy (erwin1963: kaf affrication written چ); geminate لّ

sentence: اِشْتَرَيت ثَلَث كِيلُو طَمَاطَة مِن اِلسُّوگ بِخَمْسَة آلَاف.
raw: اشتريت ثلث كيلو طماطة من السوگ بخمسة آلاف.
ipa: ʔiʃtaˈreːt ˈθalaθ ˈtʃiːluː tˤɑˈmɑːtˤɑ ˈmin isˈsuːɡ biˈxamsa ʔaːˈlaːf
gloss_en: I bought three kilos of tomatoes from the market for five thousand.
notes: interdental ث retained /θ/ (blanc1964: gilit retains interdentals); سُوگ with گ=/ɡ/ (erwin1963 orthography)

sentence: هَاذَا اِلْمُعَلِّم اِلْجِدِيد ظَلّ يِحْچِي وِيَّانَا لِلظُّهُر.
raw: هاذا المعلم الجديد ظل يحچي ويانا للظهر.
ipa: ˈhaːðaː ilmuˈʕallim ildʒiˈdiːd ˈðˤɑll ˈjiħtʃiː wijˈjaːnaː ˈliðˤðˤuhur
gloss_en: This new teacher kept talking with us until noon.
notes: ذ retained /ð/ in hāða; ظ=/ðˤ/ retained (blanc1964); يِحْچِي 'talks' with چ (erwin1963 s.v. حچى); wiyyā- 'with' (erwin1963)

sentence: گُوم جِيب الْجَاي وِالْخُبُز مِن الْمَطْبَخ.
raw: گوم جيب الجاي والخبز من المطبخ.
ipa: ˈɡuːm ˈdʒiːb alˈdʒaːj ˈwilxubuz ˈmin alˈmɑtˤbax
gloss_en: Get up, bring the tea and the bread from the kitchen.
notes: gilit imperatives guum/jiib with the dialectal گ spelling (blanc1964; erwin1963 s.v. gaam); chaay/xubuz household register

sentence: اِلْيَوم اِلْجَوّ مُو بَارِد، بَسّ مَاكُو شَمْس.
raw: اليوم الجو مو بارد، بس ماكو شمس.
ipa: ilˈjoːm ilˈdʒaww ˈmuː ˈbaːrid ˈbass ˈmaːkuː ˈʃams
gloss_en: Today the weather is not cold, but there is no sun.
notes: mu (مو) predicate negation + maku existential (erwin1963); ج=/dʒ/ retained (blanc1964); geminates جَوّ, بَسّ

gloss_en: How much is the meat today? Two thousand dinars.
notes: beesh 'how much' + il-laham (erwin1963); sun-assim اللَّحَم written with shadda (blanc1964)

gloss_en: The appointment is tomorrow at nine.
notes: baačir 'tomorrow' with چ (erwin1963); aw→oː in مَوعِد (erwin1963); sun-assim السَّاعَة (blanc1964)

gloss_en: We live with the family in Baghdad.
notes: iḥna, niskun, wiyya, ahal 'family' gilit lexicon (erwin1963)

gloss_en: Go straight and turn to the right.
notes: ruuḥ/siida/liff imperatives, 'a l-yimna 'to the right' (erwin1963); proclitic عَ + article

gloss_en: The maqluba was very good yesterday.
notes: čaanat 'was' — lexicalised kaf affrication written چ per primary-source README (blanc1964 §3.25); hwaaya 'very' (erwin1963)

sentence: مِنَين أَجِيب الْمَفَاتِيح؟ مَاكُو شِي هْنَا.
raw: منين أجيب المفاتيح؟ ماكو شي هنا.
ipa: miˈneːn ʔaˈdʒiːb almafaːˈtiːħ ˈmaːkuː ˈʃiː ˈhnaː
gloss_en: Where do I get the keys from? There is nothing here.
notes: minein 'from where' + maku negation (erwin1963); ay→eː in مِنَين (blanc1964)

gloss_en: How many children do you have at home?
notes: čam 'how many' with چ (erwin1963); ay→eː بَيت (blanc1964)

sentence: إِذَا تِجِي بَاچِر، نْرُوح سَوَة لِلسُّوگ.
raw: إذا تجي باچر، نروح سوة للسوگ.
ipa: ˈʔiðaː ˈtidʒiː ˈbaːtʃir ˈnruːħ ˈsawa lisˈsuːɡ
gloss_en: If you come tomorrow, we will go together to the market.
notes: idha/tiji/nruuḥ/sawa (erwin1963); interdental ذ retained /ð/ (blanc1964); sun-assim لِلسُّوگ

sentence: الْبَارْحَة اِشْتَرَيت خُبُز وْلَحَم مِن السُّوگ.
raw: البارحة اشتريت خبز ولحم من السوگ.
ipa: alˈbaːrħa ʔiʃtaˈreːt ˈxubuz ˈwlaħam ˈmin asˈsuːɡ
gloss_en: Yesterday I bought bread and meat from the market.
notes: khubuz 'bread' [xubuz] cf [χʊbʊz] (jasim2020); qaf→ɡ سُوگ written گ (blanc1964)

gloss_en: We will travel to Basra tomorrow by car.
notes: raḥ future + nsaafir; emphatic backing /a/→[ɑ] in بَصْرَة (jasim2020); baačir چ (erwin1963)

gloss_en: He called me on the phone yesterday.
notes: khaabar 'to phone' (erwin1963); sun-assim التِّلِفُون written with shadda (blanc1964)

gloss_en: Hello, how are things?
notes: marḥaba greeting + shlon il-ḥaal adjacency-pair opener (erwin1963)

gloss_en: One hand does not clap.
notes: proverb; ma verbal negation; emphatic ص backing (jasim2020); qaf→ɡ تْصَفُّگ written گ (blanc1964)
